text: When the user says "ascii galaxy", "the void", or "/void" (formerly "/galaxy") they mean the pi TUI landing-page extension ~/.pi/agent/extensions/void-blackhole.ts, not the ~/Developer/galaxy website (which is the lensed-blackhole personal site).
When spawning pi programmatically (RPC/print/json mode) for tests, pass --write, because the read-only-default extension disables edit/write tools in every new session.
In pi read-only/confirm mode, avoid any `>` in bash commands (including `2>/dev/null`) because the write-gate extension's redirect regex flags all redirections as destructive and blocks the command.

When a subagent reports file state that conflicts with the lead's earlier read, re-verify the actual file content directly before ruling — files can change mid-session and the lead's memory of a file goes stale.

When resizing WORDMARK or other fixed-width art in a pi setHeader render, update every width guard to measure the art (max line length) — hardcoded thresholds cause TUI uncaughtException crashes when a rendered line exceeds terminal width.

If request_write_mode reports "not found" but exists in the extension file on disk, the pi process loaded a stale copy (extensions load at session start) — dispatch the subagent call anyway, since the write-gate's subagent tool_call hook still pops the UI menu to switch to write mode; never fall back to asking in prose.

Pi's TUI stacks header + chat + custom component + footer into one buffer and pins the viewport to the bottom N terminal rows, so a full-screen landing component must keep the header at zero rows while it owns the screen (install tall headers only after the component closes) — reserve tweaks inside the component cannot compensate for header lines above it.

To run the void-blackhole extension's TS harness, use `node --experimental-strip-types --experimental-transform-types _void_harness.mts args...` instead of `npx tsx`, because tsx's CJS interop hook throws ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED on the pure-ESM @earendil-works/pi-coding-agent package.

validate-config.py in ~/.pi/agent does not schema-check theme JSON files (no theme schema in schema/manifest.json), so its "0 errors" does not vouch for theme integrity — verify themes independently (JSON parse + token parity against the sibling variant).

When the active lead model is claude-sonnet-5 (or any non-fable/non-opus model), don't narrate a delegate-vs-direct decision — the Delegation Gate section doesn't apply at all, so just do the work without mentioning subagents.
When the user says "status bar" about pi's TUI, they mean the prompt input/editor bar (the box you type in), not the footer line.

When authoring agent frontmatter, always double-quote the description value — an unquoted ": " inside it breaks YAML parsing and poisons the entire subagent loader, deadlocking all delegation until manually repaired.

Benchmarked at ~500-line multi-workstream scale (spreadsheet engine, 68 hidden tests): all four configs passed 68/68 including integration tests, blind review ranked fable-solo (9/10) above the full pipeline (8/10) at half the cost ($2.76 vs $5.68), with quality tracking model tier not orchestration (sonnet had real spec-corner defects at 5/10) — so up to single-session task sizes, pay for a stron

When a verified fix in usage-dashboard "doesn't work", check first whether the user is running the packaged Electron app from release/ — source edits only reach it after `npm run app:dist` AND an app relaunch (main.js re-extracts standalone.tar when the bundled tar is newer than the extracted server.js).

When a pi extension reports "shortcut conflicts with built-in, skipping", check ~/.pi/agent/keybindings.json first — a user rebinding of a built-in action to that key causes the conflict, and removing the rebind (reverting to the action's default key) is the fix rather than changing the extension's shortcut.
When the user gives a list of tasks or multiple distinct tasks in one message, use the task tool (add/list/toggle) to track them instead of just replying inline.

Before re-fixing a bug already described in the heuristics store, check recent session logs — a prior fix may have been reverted by a later "undo all changes" request, silently reintroducing the same bug.

In pi TUI setHeader components, always truncateToWidth() every returned line even when a width guard already exists — a stale/raced width param can bypass the guard and overflow the terminal, crashing the TUI; the width guard alone is not sufficient.

Pi's startup [Skills]/[Prompts]/[Extensions]/[Themes] sections are rendered by showLoadedResources() in dist/modes/interactive/interactive-mode.js via addLoadedSection() calls — to hide a specific section, comment out just that section's addLoadedSection call (keeps others and Context/diagnostics intact); re-apply after any pi package reinstall/update since dist/ is regenerated.

The ~/.pi/agent launchd autocommit daemon runs the pre-commit validate-config.py gate under launchd's minimal PATH (/usr/bin/python3, which lacks jsonschema), so schema violations silently degrade to parse-only, exit 0, and get auto-committed — the pre-commit "backstop" only truly blocks for interactive commits from a shell whose python3 has jsonschema.

When testing git-commit gating in ~/.pi/agent, the launchd autocommit daemon is WatchPath-triggered on the whole dir (settings.json/keybindings.json/AGENTS.md) and commits any file edit within ~10s — so test the pre-commit hook by invoking .pi-vcs/hooks/pre-commit directly with the file staged (race-immune) rather than via `git commit`, which the daemon will sweep mid-block.
When modifying pi message-queue behavior, preserve native Enter-to-send-or-steer and Option+Enter-to-queue-follow-up unless explicitly asked otherwise.

The graphify `query` action anchors lexically on literal tokens in the question (a vague word like "result" BFS-walks unrelated variables), so use `explain` with a concrete node name or phrase queries around distinctive identifiers.

In pi (verified 0.80.3), pi.sendUserMessage("/cmd") does NOT dispatch the command despite the docs' reload_runtime example — prompt() gates command dispatch behind expandPromptTemplates, which sendUserMessage hard-sets false, and delivered follow-ups drain straight to the LLM via the agent-loop bypassing prompt(); reload()/command dispatch is reachable only from a user-typed slash command or Exten

In pi 0.80.3, extensions cannot programmatically dispatch slash commands: sendUserMessage() hard-sets expandPromptTemplates:false so "/cmd" text goes to the LLM verbatim, and ctx.reload() exists only on command contexts — auto-reload from event hooks is infeasible despite the extensions.md reload_runtime example suggesting otherwise.

The pi graph tool (graphify-bridge) resolves graphify-out by walking up from cwd with a fallback to the agent config dir's graph — a cwd with its own graphify-out always wins, so from unrelated directories graph answers describe the ~/.pi/agent config, not the local project.
After editing loaded resources (extensions, skills, prompts, themes, AGENTS.md) in ~/.pi/agent, run /refresh (or /reload) so pi picks up the changes instead of running on a stale in-memory copy.

When QA'ing pi extensions, have the QA subagent live-fire them in its own session — extensions load in spawned subagent sessions too, so the dispatch itself becomes the integration test (a graph-first block was observed live in-run this way).
When spawning pi subagents on claude-sonnet-5 or gemini-3.5-flash, pin thinking to high instead of inheriting the parent effort.
